purpose: Concrete examples for LF gamification safety controls. These examples train repair or block decisions without adding a new layer.

examples.risk_pattern: copy frames the user negatively or reduces emotional safety
examples.blocked_variant: message rewards only users who act under emotional pressure
examples.safe_repair: replace with supportive neutral copy and clear optional next step

examples.risk_pattern: mission uses artificial scarcity or unsupported deadline
examples.blocked_variant: reward expires for reasons not grounded in the product reality
examples.safe_repair: remove artificial scarcity and explain the real condition if one exists

examples.risk_pattern: progress compares users by sensitive financial status
examples.blocked_variant: leaderboard or badge exposes status-based comparison
examples.safe_repair: replace with private progress, personal milestones and educational completion signals

examples.risk_pattern: streak loss removes dignity or creates avoidable stress
examples.blocked_variant: missed day causes loss framing without recovery
examples.safe_repair: add recovery path, grace state and supportive restart copy

examples.risk_pattern: reward can be confused with financial value or guaranteed benefit
examples.blocked_variant: points look like money, savings or eligibility without explanation
examples.safe_repair: mark reward as symbolic or educational and state what it does not mean

traceability.internal_lf: LF clarity, wellbeing, no-pressure and traceability controls
traceability.own_repo: Quality Pack gates and UI Architect example pattern
traceability.reason_for_location: anti-pattern examples make blocking and repair criteria concrete
